content:
{"selectedIdeas":[{"title":"Live Task Arena — Public Leaderboard with Real-Time Bot Battles","description":"A public-facing dashboard that visualizes Clawbot tasks executing in real-time: which bots are competing for tasks, who claimed them, escrow amounts locked, and final verified outcomes. Think a stock ticker meets a bounty board. Users can watch bots race to complete tasks, see dispute resolutions unfold, and track top-earning verified bots over time. The WebSocket infrastructure (already authenticated per BUG-CRIT-001) makes this naturally real-time. This transforms an opaque backend marketplace into a spectator sport — making the platform feel alive even before you're a participant.","viralPotential":"Shareable 'My bot just earned $X solving Y task in Z seconds' achievement cards auto-generated after each completed task. Leaderboard screenshots are inherently shareable. Developers will post their bot's ranking on Twitter/X. The 'watch mode' URL is linkable — someone can share a live task in progress.","mvpScope":"1. WebSocket broadcast of sanitized task lifecycle events (claim → execution → verification → escrow release) to an unauthenticated public feed. 2. A single-page React dashboard: active tasks list, top 10 bots by earnings this week, last 20 completed tasks with outcome + payout. 3. Auto-generated PNG achievement card endpoint (bot name, task type, earnings, timestamp). No auth required to view. Ship in 8–10 days.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Live Arena Feed: Full-width ticker of task events scrolling in real-time (task posted → bot claimed → evidence submitted → verified ✓ / disputed ⚠). Color-coded by task category. Escrow amount shown as a glowing badge.","Screen 2 — Bot Leaderboard: Top bots ranked by 7-day / 30-day / all-time earnings. Each row shows bot alias, task count, success rate, total earned. Clicking a bot shows its task history.","Screen 3 — Achievement Card Generator: Auto-rendered shareable card with dark gradient background, bot name, task summary, payout amount, and a QR code linking back to the task record on the marketplace."],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":null},{"title":"Verified Reputation Passport — On-Chain-Style Bot Identity with Portable Trust Score","description":"Each bot registered on the marketplace gets a publicly linkable 'Passport' page: a cryptographically signed summary of its task history, dispute win/loss ratio, evidence quality score (derived from the existing evidence filtering logic), and a tiered trust badge (Bronze → Silver → Gold → Verified Elite). The passport is generated from the existing escrow + dispute + policy enforcement data already in the system. Critically, it's exportable as a signed JSON blob so bot operators can embed their trust score in external profiles, GitHub READMEs, or other marketplaces. This directly monetizes the verification infrastructure already built.","viralPotential":"Bot operators will embed their passport badge in GitHub READMEs, personal sites, and job applications ('I built a Gold-tier verified autonomous agent'). The signed JSON export means the credential travels outside your platform — every embed is a brand impression. Competitive operators will grind for tier upgrades, driving task volume.","mvpScope":"1. Passport page at /bots/:id/passport — pulls from existing task/escrow/dispute tables, computes score server-side. 2. Tier calculation logic (simple weighted formula: task count, success rate, dispute outcomes, evidence quality flags). 3. Embeddable SVG badge with tier + score (one GET endpoint, no auth). 4. Signed JSON export endpoint — HMAC-signed snapshot of the passport data for portability. No new data collection needed — purely derived from existing records. Ship in 7–9 days.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Bot Passport Page: Hero section with bot alias, tier badge (animated shimmer for Gold+), join date, and headline stats (tasks completed, success rate, total earned, disputes won). Below: chronological task history with evidence quality indicators.","Screen 2 — Tier Progress Bar: Visual progress toward next tier with specific requirements shown ('12 more tasks at 95%+ success rate to reach Gold'). Motivates continued engagement.","Screen 3 — Export & Embed Panel: One-click copy of embeddable SVG badge code, download signed JSON passport, and a shareable passport URL. Preview shows how the badge renders on a dark and light background."],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":null},{"title":"Task Autopsy — Public Post-Mortem Feed for Failed & Disputed Tasks","description":"After a task dispute resolves (using the already-hardened dispute target validation and policy enforcement), automatically publish a redacted 'autopsy' report: what the task required, what evidence was submitted, why it failed verification or triggered a dispute, and what the policy engine decided. This is educational content that builds developer trust in the marketplace's fairness — showing the rules are real and enforced consistently. Bot builders learn what evidence quality looks like, reducing future disputes. It also surfaces the OPA policy (marketplace.rego) as a human-readable explainer rather than an opaque black box.","viralPotential":"Developers love post-mortems. 'Why my bot got slashed' threads on Hacker News/Reddit are extremely high-engagement. Each autopsy is a standalone shareable URL. A weekly 'Disputed Tasks Digest' email/RSS feed gives the marketplace a media presence beyond just being infrastructure. Security researchers will reference the policy explainers.","mvpScope":"1. After dispute resolution, write a structured autopsy record: task type, evidence fields present/missing, policy rules triggered (pulled from OPA decision log), final verdict, escrow outcome. Redact bot identifiers if operator opts out. 2. Public feed at /autopsies — paginated list of resolved disputes, filterable by task category and verdict type. 3. Individual autopsy page with a plain-English policy explanation ('Rule marketplace.evidence_completeness fired because field X was absent'). 4. RSS feed endpoint. Ship in 8–10 days.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Autopsy Feed: Card grid of recent dispute resolutions. Each card shows task category icon, verdict badge (Upheld / Overturned / Partial), escrow amount at stake, and a one-line plain-English summary of the failure reason. Filterable sidebar.","Screen 2 — Autopsy Detail Page: Timeline view of the task lifecycle with annotated failure points highlighted in red. Policy rules that fired are shown as expandable human-readable blocks (not raw Rego). Evidence checklist showing what was present vs. required.","Screen 3 — Policy Explorer (bonus): Side panel showing the full set of policy rules in plain English, with a counter of how many times each rule has been the deciding factor across all disputes. Makes the governance system transparent and legible."],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":null}],"ideationPlan":"Chairman synthesis failed. Top ideas selected by score.","gitAnalysis":{"repoName":"Clawbot-marketplace","description":"Production-oriented implementation scaffold for a public verified Clawbot task marketplace with:","techStack":["TypeScript"],"recentCommits":["e9f0059 fix(security+hardening): BUG-HIGH-001 CORS, BUG-MED-001 timing-safe lease, BUG-MED-002 evidence filtering, BUG-HIGH-003 dispute target validation, BUG-MED-003 policy enforcement — 314 tests passing","47972df fix(api): authenticate WebSocket endpoints (BUG-CRIT-001)","a227347 feat(security+integrity): TASK-HARD-007/008/010 + TASK-FEAT-001/006 + fix pre-existing test failures","c5e9fea arch: comprehensive bug inventory — 14 untracked bugs + fix task definitions","bc3fb14 test(api): comprehensive endpoint and integration test coverage","4e16581 arch: comprehensive architecture deep-dive — Moltbook, escrow, state machines, security","7d4654b test(escrow): add comprehensive escrow and ledger test coverage (TASK-HARD-006)","00a1ba0 init: clawbot marketplace v1 alpha baseline"],"fileStructure":"./.DS_Store

{"selectedIdeas":[{"title":"Rupee-Cost Transparency Dashboard ("AI Meter")","description":"A real-time cost tracker that shows exactly how many rupees each API call costs, with a running monthly burn-rate, model-vs-model cost comparisons, and a 'what would this cost on OpenAI/Anthropic?' side-by-side. India-first angle: displays costs in ₹ with UPI-style micro-transaction feel. Developers instantly see savings from using Indian inference vs global clouds.","viralPotential":"Developers love sharing 'I saved ₹12,000 this month vs GPT-4' screenshots on Twitter/LinkedIn. The comparison widget is inherently shareable — 'AI Bazaar vs OpenAI cost' is a search term waiting to happen. Finance teams at startups will forward the dashboard to CTOs.","mvpScope":"Single dashboard page pulling from existing usage logs. Show: (1) total tokens used today/week/month, (2) cost in ₹ with live exchange-rate conversion, (3) static comparison table of 3-4 rival providers for the same model class. No new backend needed if usage data already exists — pure frontend aggregation + a hardcoded comparison table that's manually updated weekly.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Meter Overview: Big ₹ number front-and-center (today's spend), sparkline graph of last 30 days, 'You saved ₹X vs GPT-4 this month' green badge, quick model breakdown donut chart","Screen 2 — Model Comparison Table: Rows = models available on AI Bazaar, columns = AI Bazaar ₹/1K tokens | OpenAI equivalent | Anthropic equivalent | % saving. CTA 'Switch to cheaper model' inline per row","Screen 3 — Shareable Report Card: Auto-generated PNG/OG-image card showing monthly stats, savings badge, and AI Bazaar branding — one-click share to Twitter/LinkedIn"],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":8.666666666666666},{"title":"Model API Key "Bazaar Pass" — Shareable Team Spending Links","description":"A viral growth mechanic disguised as a team feature. Any user can generate a 'Bazaar Pass' — a budget-limited, model-scoped, expiry-dated API sub-key — and share it as a link or QR code. Use cases: a founder gives their developer a ₹500/month pass scoped to one model; a hackathon organizer gives 200 participants a 24-hour pass; a freelancer shares a demo pass with a client. Each pass has its own analytics page visible to the creator.","viralPotential":"Every shared Bazaar Pass is a branded touchpoint. Recipients land on a page that says 'Powered by AI Bazaar — get your own account'. Hackathon organizers become distribution channels — one organizer = 200 new users seeing the platform. The QR code format is perfect for in-person events like college hackathons and developer meetups, which are massive in India.","mvpScope":"Build on top of existing API key infrastructure: (1) a 'Create Pass' form (budget ₹ cap, model whitelist, expiry date, optional label), (2) generates a sub-key stored in DB linked to parent account, (3) a public pass-info page (no auth needed) showing pass name, remaining budget, expiry — with 'Create your own' CTA, (4) parent dashboard shows all active passes + spend per pass. 1-week backend, 3-day frontend.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Create Pass Form: Inputs: Pass name, ₹ budget limit, model scope (multi-select checkboxes), expiry date picker, optional 'note to recipient'. Preview card updates live. 'Generate Pass' button produces link + QR code.","Screen 2 — Pass Public Landing Page (unauthenticated): Clean card showing pass name, issuer (e.g. 'From Rahul at StartupXYZ'), models available, budget remaining as progress bar, expiry countdown. Copy API key button + 'Get your own AI Bazaar account' banner at bottom.","Screen 3 — Pass Management Dashboard: Table of all created passes — name, recipient label, budget used/total, models, status (active/expired/depleted), revoke button. Click any row to see per-pass usage timeline graph."],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":8.333333333333334},{"title":""Bharat Prompt Playground" — Multilingual Zero-Setup Tester","description":"An in-browser prompt playground that works across 10 Indian languages (Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, Marathi, Kannada, Malayalam, Gujarati, Punjabi, Odia) with one-click language switching, Devanagari/regional script keyboard input, and instant model comparison side-by-side. No API key required for the first 50 free requests (phone-number verified via OTP). This directly targets India's massive non-English developer and enterprise market that global playgrounds ignore.","viralPotential":"First time most Indian developers see an AI tool that actually works well in their native language AND is built by an Indian company. 'Finally an AI that understands Tamil' posts will spread organically. The free OTP tier means zero friction to try — every WhatsApp forward converts to a user. Regional tech communities (Tamil Nadu Developers, etc.) will champion it.","mvpScope":"Extend a basic chat UI with: (1) language selector dropdown with 10 Indian languages, (2) Google Input Tools or a simple transliteration library for script input, (3) side-by-side A/B model comparison (pick Model A vs Model B, same prompt, see both outputs), (4) OTP-gated free tier using existing auth infrastructure. No new models needed — just the UI wrapper around existing inference API.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Playground Home: Two-column layout (Model A | Model B), language selector pill at top, prompt textarea with script-toggle button (Roman ↔ Native script), big 'Run on both models' CTA button, latency + cost shown per response","Screen 2 — OTP Free-Tier Gate: Clean modal — 'Try free, no credit card' → phone number input → OTP → unlocks 50 requests. Shows remaining free credits as a pill in the nav permanently after login","Screen 3 — Response View: Side-by-side output cards with model name, response time badge, token count, ₹ cost chip, and a 'This one was better 👍' vote button that feeds anonymous quality data back to the platform"],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":8}],"ideationPlan":"Chairman synthesis failed. Top ideas selected by score.","gitAnalysis":{"repoName":"AI-Bazaar","description":"AI Bazaar is an India-first AI marketplace and inference platform.","techStack":["Next.js","React","Tailwind CSS","TypeScript"],"recentCommits":["5e2a81a chore: final supervision cycle — 88/88 tasks DONE, all tests passing","f2119f2 fix: resolve Vercel build failure — API_KEY_HASH_SALT crash during page-data collection","afa4b68 fix: resolve Vercel build failure — API_KEY_HASH_SALT crash during page-data collection","34eb7b5 docs: architect session 3 — ARCHITECTURE.md rewrite, ADR-013, TASKS.md update","b39483c fix: allow API_KEY_HASH_SALT fallback during Next.js build phase","b24f986 fix: make hashSalt lazy to fix Vercel build crash","433b025 fix: make hashSalt lazy to fix Vercel build crash","c104300 chore: final supervision cycle — 88/88 tasks DONE, 93 test files (1347 tests) passing, zero TS errors","8a6636a feat: AI Bazaar v1 complete — 88/88 tasks done, 93 test files, 1347 tests passing","c8339c2 feat: AI Bazaar project 100% complete — 88/88 tasks done, 1347 tests passing","f0ae4a9 chore: supervision cycle — project 100% complete, 88/88 tasks DONE, 1347 tests passing","4413e5b feat: AI Bazaar complete — 88/88 tasks done, 1347 tests passing","3d1f935 chore: supervision cycle — 88/88 DONE, 1347/1347 tests pass, build fully clean","d0d0f2b chore: supervision cycle — all 88 tasks DONE, 1347 tests passing, build clean","1bd29e8 chore: supervision cycle — 88/88 DONE, 1347 tests pass, all agents completed, project 100% complete","7f116ce feat: AI Bazaar 100% complete — 88/88 tasks done, 1347 tests passing","2b5fc6f chore: supervision cycle — 88/88 DONE, 93 test files, 1347 tests passing, 0 lint errors, 0 type errors","f59a739 feat: AI Bazaar v1 complete — 88/88 tasks done, 1347 tests passing","d554772 feat: AI Bazaar v1.0 — all 88 tasks complete, 1347 tests passing","33652ac chore: supervision cycle — all 88 tasks DONE, 1347 tests passing, build fully clean"],"fileStructure":"./.vercel/project.json
